video:video.video:title: Troy Baker stars in Netflix game 'Unhinged' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Troy Baker is an icon in the video game world, with lead roles as a voice and performance capture actor in "The Last of Us" and "BioShock Infinite" and villain turns in "Far Cry 4" and "Batman: Arkham Asylum."

video:video.video:title: Inside Wimbledon's queue, where fans wait hours for last-minute tickets
video:video.video:description: Wimbledon is one of the few major sporting events where fans can get tickets in person on the day of the match.

video:video.video:title: We asked children who taught them about money
video:video.video:description: We asked children who taught them about money

video:video.video:title: Tips to talk about money with children
video:video.video:description: Personal finance expert Carrie Joy Grimes shares tips for parents to talk about money with their children

video:video.video:title: Trump warns 'godless communists' taking over Democratic party are 'most serious threat' to US
video:video.video:description: Seeking to energize his base ahead of November's midterm elections, President Donald Trump warned that "godless communists" taking over the Democratic Party pose "the most serious threat" to the U.S. since its founding as he addressed the Faith & Freedom Coalition.

video:video.video:title: Funeral for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at Najaf airport in Iraq
video:video.video:description: An official funeral ceremony was held Tuesday night for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at Najaf International airport in Iraq.

video:video.video:title: Sightings of humpback whales surge in Rio de Janeiro, fueling demand for whale-watching trips
video:video.video:description: Sightings of humpback whales off Rio de Janeiro’s coast are surging as they recover from decimation due to commercial whaling, prompting an acceleration in the demand for whale-watching excursions to spot the huge marine creatures during their annual migration.

video:video.video:title: 'The building remains unstable,' New York mayor says about Manhattan building
video:video.video:description: A Manhattan high-rise under construction buckled and bricks tumbled into the street below; Mayor Mamdani says no injuries have been reported.

video:video.video:title: Blackout hits Cuba as fuel reserve dwindles and aging grid crumbles
video:video.video:description: An island-wide blackout hit Cuba on Monday as fuel reserves dwindled and the electric grid continued to crumble.

video:video.video:title: AP Is There: Taylor Swift's Wedding at Madison Square Garden
video:video.video:description: Today is a fairytale! Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce got married and AP was there.

video:video.video:title: Renewable energy and coal coexist in Inner Mongolia's electricity plan
video:video.video:description: China's Inner Mongolia is expanding both renewable energy and coal use. Despite the growth of solar and wind farms, coal remains essential.

video:video.video:title: Egg producers will pay $3.3M and donate 53 million eggs to settle price fixing claims
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Justice Department and 17 states have reached settlement agreements with three major egg producers over allegations that the companies illegally colluded in a multiyear scheme to raises prices.

video:video.video:title: UK and Norwegian PMs shake hands ahead of their countries' World Cup quarter final match
video:video.video:description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre posed for photos in their countries' football shirts in Turkey on Tuesday, ahead of the FIFA World Cup quarter final clash between England and Norway on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Stars watch fantasy and florals at Chanel
video:video.video:description: Elizabeth Debicki, Anna Wintour and Baz Luhrmann among the guests watching Chanel's Fall-Winter 2026-2027 show, at Paris Haute Couture Week. (July 7)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 7
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday July 7th: Democrats pulling endorsements for Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner; Trump heads to NATO summit in Turkey; Tanker hit by projectile, catches fire in Strait of Hormuz; U.S.

video:video.video:title: Joy turns to disappointment for USA World Cup fans from coast to coast
video:video.video:description: USA World Cup soccer fans rode an emotional roller coaster as Monday night's game against Belgium got off to a promising start but ended with a 4-1 loss.

video:video.video:title: Buckling beams found inside Manhattan building under construction prompting evacuations
video:video.video:description: Reports of falling bricks and buckling columns have prompted evacuations in the area around a Midtown Manhattan high-rise office building that was being converted to luxury apartments, fire officials said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Explosions rock Damascus as French President meets with Syrian counterpart
video:video.video:description: It was the second blast to rock the capital in a few days, and a setback for the country’s new president as he welcomed his first visit from a western leader since ousting longtime dictator Bashar Assad.

video:video.video:title: Bodycam footage shows officers arresting a couple after climbing the Empire State Building
video:video.video:description: Bodycam footage shows officers arresting two people after scaling the Empire State Building’s antenna and unfurling a banner about “the power of love.”

video:video.video:title: Trump takes first flight on new Air Force One gifted by Qatar
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump celebrated the new Qatari-gifted Air Force One Wednesday before its initial voyage to North Dakota, saying the U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 6
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, July 6th: Preliminary hearing held for man accused of killing Charlie Kirk; Important U.S. historical documents arrive in Michigan; Trump touts Trump Accounts for children as he rings in New York Stock Exchange; Lego unveils massive World Cup trophy replica in Manhattan

video:video.video:title: Bodies brought to Gaza morgue after deadly Israeli airstrikes
video:video.video:description: On Monday, Israeli strikes killed at least five people in Gaza, including three in Khan Younis in the south and two in an apartment in Gaza City, health officials said.

video:video.video:title: Protesters clash with police outside Albania's parliament
video:video.video:description: Protesters clashed with police on Thursday outside Albania's Parliament as officers used tear gas and a water cannon to disperse crowds and arrested dozens.

video:video.video:title: Le Pen's possible stand-in as presidential candidate is no carbon copy
video:video.video:description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen's political future is on the line as a Paris appeals court rules on her eligibility to stand in the 2027 presidential election.

video:video.video:title: 2 people arrested after hanging a banner about 'the power of love' on Empire State Building antenna
video:video.video:description: Two people got to the top of the Empire State Building's antenna and unfurled a banner about “the power of love” at midday Wednesday, before descending, embracing, taking selfies and ultimately being arrested.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he called FIFA president to review US red card: 'That wasn't a foul'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said he called FIFA President Gianni Infantino to ask for a review of the red card issued to U.S. men's national team striker Folarin Balogun, arguing the collision wasn’t a foul and calling the referee "very suspect."

video:video.video:title: Fans celebrate BTS and Korean culture
video:video.video:description: BTS fans flocked to the Korean Cultural Centre in London ahead of the group's UK gigs for screenings, photo opps and BTS' stage outfits - plus to try on some traditional Korean outfits.

video:video.video:title: Indigenous food producer hopes Farm Bill will restore programs
video:video.video:description: Dawn and Cassius Spears would like to expand their Rhode Island farm, but the Trump Administration has cut or scaled back some federal programs they and other small producers relied on to grow and distribute food.

video:video.video:title: FIFA lifts Folarin Balogun’s red card suspension at World Cup
video:video.video:description: The Belgian soccer federation wants an explanation from FIFA about a decision to let U.S forward Folarin Balogun play at the World Cup despite getting a red card in his previous game.

video:video.video:title: California to institute Bruce Lee Day, a first for a Chinese American in the state's history
video:video.video:description: Martial arts icon Bruce Lee, who was born in San Francisco, will become the first Chinese American in California history with an annual namesake day.

video:video.video:title: UK government apologizes for the state's role in decades of forced adoptions
video:video.video:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has formally apologized for the British state's role in separating unmarried mothers from their babies.

video:video.video:title: Trump arrives at NATO summit in Turkey
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump arrived in Turkey on Tuesday to meet with fellow NATO leaders. The summit comes as the alliance tries to persuade him that their countries are actively increasing their military capabilities even as the American focus shifts away from its defense of Europe toward Asia.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers pull the injured from rubble in Kyiv after Russian attack
video:video.video:description: Russian missiles and drones kill at least 8 people in Kyiv, causing damage across Ukrainian capital

video:video.video:title: A digital only, hyper-local volunteer team focuses on severe weather forecasting in Nashville
video:video.video:description: When tornadoes threaten in Nashville, Tennessee, many people turn to Nashville Severe Weather, a group of hyper-local and digital only forecasters.

video:video.video:title: Manhattan high-rise is still unstable after columns buckle, forcing evacuations
video:video.video:description: An under-construction high-rise in Manhattan was still unstable Tuesday after buckling columns and sagging floors raised fears of a collapse, forcing the tower and other nearby buildings to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Four adults charged after 16 children found in 'deplorable' conditions in southern Ohio home
video:video.video:description: The children are part of the same family, officials said. The four people charged appeared in court Wednesday where a judge entered not guilty pleas on their behalf.

video:video.video:title: Brazil fans heartbroken after World Cup exit as Norway celebrates Haaland
video:video.video:description: Norway fans were beside themselves as they left the stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey, on Sunday after their team made it to the World Cup quarterfinals for the very first time by beating Brazil 2-1.

video:video.video:title: FIFA to investigate racism towards IShowSpeed at World Cup
video:video.video:description: FIFA launches an investigation into racism toward popular streamer IShowSpeed in the stands during the World Cup match between Argentina and Cape Verde.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 7
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, July 7th: New York high-rise buckles, triggers evacuations; explosions injure 18 in Syria during Macron visit; militant attack kills 9 in Pakistan; deadly tropical storm in China forces thousands to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 4
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, July 4th: Funeral begins for late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei; Protest is held in Germany against far-right party; Ukrainian firefighters fight blaze in Chernobyl; United States celebrates it's 250th anniversary of independence.

video:video.video:title: Trump hails US exceptionalism before veering into darkly political speech to usher in America 250
video:video.video:description: At Mount Rushmore on Friday, Trump spoke of communism as a "mortal threat to American liberty.”

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy makes case for Ukraine to be granted Patriot license
video:video.video:description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Monday that if Ukraine is granted a license to manufacture Patriot missiles, it could protect the country and its neighbors, saying the main challenge is a shortage of interceptors.

video:video.video:title: Why losing weight isn't just about counting calories
video:video.video:description: Here's why the simple math of counting calories doesn’t always add up when it comes to losing weight.

video:video.video:title: Funerals of children killed in roof collapse at tutoring centre under construction in Lahore
video:video.video:description: Mourners gathered Wednesday in Pakistan’s eastern city of Lahore to bury 14 schoolchildren who were killed when the roof of a tutoring center collapsed on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Rare copy of Declaration of Independence found by UK National Archives in papers of captured US ship
video:video.video:description: The National Archives in London discovered a rare 1776 printing of the U.S. Declaration of Independence. (AP Video shot by Kwiyeon Ha)

video:video.video:title: AP reporter describes severe weather disrupting DC's America 250th celebration
video:video.video:description: Severe weather disrupted celebrations of America's 250th anniversary of independence Saturday in several East Coast cities, including Washington, where storms prompted a roughly two-hour evacuation of the National Mall.

video:video.video:title: Fans gather outside MSG for Taylor Swift wedding
video:video.video:description: Excitement builds outside Madison Square Garden, as well-wishing fans look for clues about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding. (July 3)

video:video.video:title: What to know as a Manhattan high-rise is unstable after columns buckle, forcing evacuations
video:video.video:description: A Manhattan high-rise remained unstable Tuesday after officials found that columns buckled and floors sagged while it was being converted to luxury apartments, forcing evacuations in and around the Midtown construction site.

video:video.video:title: Why a red card is every World Cup player’s nightmare
video:video.video:description: A single red card can completely change a World Cup match. Here’s why it’s the most feared punishment in soccer. Produced by Nandini Gupt

video:video.video:title: Democratic states urged to pull Guard troops from Washington
video:video.video:description: The National Guard deployment in Washington, D.C. ballooned during America 250 celebrations, bolstered in part by contingents from Democratic-led states.

video:video.video:title: Runners in high heels dash through Madrid neighborhood for annual Pride event.
video:video.video:description: Madrid's iconic high heel race returned to the streets of Chueca as part of the city's annual LGBTQ+ Pride celebrations.

video:video.video:title: As war grinds on, Ukrainian climbers build a new outdoor culture inspired by Yosemite
video:video.video:description: The Stoned Climbers festival, which took place last week in the Zhytomyr region, is Ukraine’s biggest outdoors event combining climbing and live music.

video:video.video:title: Venezuela's acting president defends government response to double earthquake
video:video.video:description: Venezuela's Acting President Delcy Rodríguez late Thursday pushed back against complaints that the government responded too slowly to the disaster and left civilians to shoulder most of the rescue efforts.

video:video.video:title: Seaplane lands in New York City's East River, no injuries reported
video:video.video:description: The sea plane carrying 10 people made a “hard landing” around noon, according to the New York City Fire Department.

video:video.video:title: Hugh Grant, Ethan Hawke, Jason Sudeikis arrive at Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wedding
video:video.video:description: Hollywood stars and pro athletes were among the guests who made their way to Madison Square Garden to witness Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce tie the knot. (July 3)

video:video.video:title: Democrats begin pulling Platner endorsements after Maine candidate faces sexual assault allegation
video:video.video:description: A woman Platner previously dated accused him of drunkenly forcing her to have sex with him after she told him to stop according to a Politico report.

video:video.video:title: Island-wide blackout hits Cuba as its fuel reserve dwindles
video:video.video:description: Fuel has been running out across Cuba since January when U.S. President Donald Trump threatened tariffs on any country that sells or provides oil to the island, deepening the island’s ongoing economic and financial crisis.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 2
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday July 2nd: Massive Russian attack on Kyiv, Ukraine; Progress reported in U.S.

video:video.video:title: Panini sticker swapping continues during 2026 World Cup
video:video.video:description: More than 50 years since their creation, Panini World Cup stickers continue to bring soccer fans together around the world.

video:video.video:title: Time capsule buried in Philadelphia with items from around the country as US marks 250th birthday
video:video.video:description: Dozens gathered at Independence National Historical Park in Philadelphia to bury a time capsule containing artifacts from the U.S. government and all the country's states and territories.

video:video.video:title: Waterspout hits Lake Constance in Germany
video:video.video:description: A waterspout formed over a lake in Germany in a rare weather event that caused a stir in the area. The phenomenon was seen forming over Lake Constance near Lindau on Wednesday, German news agency DPA reported.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, July 5th: Iran officials appeared at Khamenei’s funeral; firefighters battled wildfires in Portugal, Greece and Spain; a Coney Island shooting wounded eight; Ukrainian climbers build a culture inspired by Yosemite.

video:video.video:title: Defying Pope Leo XIV and risking schism, traditionalists go ahead with Latin Mass consecrations
video:video.video:description: The Society of St. Pius X, which opposes modernizing reforms in the Catholic Church, went ahead with a ritual-filled ceremony at its seminary in Econe, Switzerland, despite a last-ditch appeal by Leo to call it off.

video:video.video:title: AP journalist reports on sweltering heat wave at the Great American State Fair in DC
video:video.video:description: AP reporter Nathan Ellgren reports on the rising temperatures in the Great American State fair in Washington D.C. ahead of U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 3
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, July 3rd: Mourners file through Damascus carrying a coffin after deadly explosion; Pope Leo XIV accepts Liberty Medal; Zelenskyy honors military on Army Aviation Day in Ukraine; Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding in New York causes street closures

video:video.video:title: How AI is being used to create videos of the dead
video:video.video:description: A growing number of digitally-savvy South Koreans are experimenting with AI's ability to produce video recreations of the dead.

video:video.video:title: Supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's casket on display in Tehran ahead of dayslong funeral
video:video.video:description: A procession passed by the coffins of Iran's slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and his family on Friday, according to state media.

video:video.video:title: 'Kiki's Delivery Service' author says book was inspired by daughter's witch drawing
video:video.video:description: Eiko Kadono, the author of the novel behind Hayao Miyazaki's film "Kiki's Delivery Service," discusses her love of writing and how the book came to be. (July 1)

video:video.video:title: 2 people arrested after hanging banner about 'the power of love' on Empire State Building antenna
video:video.video:description: Two people who got to the top of the Empire State Building’s antenna and unfurled a banner about “the power of love” have been arrested.

video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out on Brooklyn Bridge during 4th of July fireworks show
video:video.video:description: Fire crews extinguished a fire on New York's Brooklyn Bridge Saturday after dueling firework shows malfunctioned over the Hudson and East Rivers.

video:video.video:title: Fantasy and floral romance at Chanel Haute Couture
video:video.video:description: Elizabeth Debicki, Anna Wintour and Baz Luhrmann among the guests watching Chanel's Fall-Winter 2026-2027 show, at Paris Haute Couture Week. (July 7)

video:video.video:title: Russia launches deadly waves of missiles and drones at Ukraine capital
video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed in the latest Russian attack, which caused a residential building in Kyiv's Podilskyi district to partially collapse.

video:video.video:title: Deadly clashes break out at prison in Sri Lanka
video:video.video:description: Deadly clashes broke out inside a prison in the outskirts of Sri Lanka’s capital, officials said on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Roof collapse at tutoring centre under construction in Lahore kills at least 14 children
video:video.video:description: A roof collapse at a tutoring center in Pakistan’s Kahna Town of Lahore District killed at least 14 schoolchildren on Tuesday, according to the police and rescue officials.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine capital hit by waves of deadly Russian missiles and drones
video:video.video:description: The attack on Kyiv which was still underway early Monday morning, involved waves of ballistic and cruise missiles as well as drones.

video:video.video:title: Vatican declares ultraconservative society in schism, excommunicates bishops and warns faithful
video:video.video:description: The Vatican responded aggressively Thursday to a traditionalist group that consecrated bishops without the pope’s consent, declaring the Society of St.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian drone attacks on oil refineries plunge Russia into a summer fuel crisis
video:video.video:description: The lines are growing at Russian gas stations -- and so is the frustration and anxiety as months of Ukrainian attacks have set oil refineries ablaze and choked supplies for motorists across the vast country.

video:video.video:title: Russia waged a drone campaign in Europe, likely using shadow ships, report says
video:video.video:description: Russia likely used shadow ships to launch drones over Europe that repeatedly disrupted civilian aviation, as it monitored military sites and tested the air defenses of NATO nations, according to a report published Thursday by the International Institute of Strategic Studies think tank.

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce to celebrate wedding at Madison Square Garden
video:video.video:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce will celebrate their wedding at Madison Square Garden on Friday night, according to a law enforcement official briefed on the security plans.

video:video.video:title: Deadly clashes in prison near Sri Lanka capital
video:video.video:description: Clashes broke out inside a prison in the outskirts of Sri Lanka’s capital, killing at least 25 people, most of them inmates, and injuring over 100, officials.

video:video.video:title: Venezuela starts burying victims of earthquakes as thousands still missing
video:video.video:description: Graves for more than 150 unidentified victims were dug at La Esperanza Cemetery on Monday. Authorities announced that the death toll rose on Monday to 3,535, with another 16,740 people injured.

video:video.video:title: Franklin Park Zoo staff work to keep animals cool during Fourth of July weekend heat
video:video.video:description: Oppressive heat is hitting the eastern U.S. ahead of the Fourth of July weekend, disrupting holiday plans.

video:video.video:title: Trump insists again that US should control Greenland
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the semiautonomous island, which is part of NATO ally Denmark, is “an important part for the United States,” and that he does not intend to let Greenland be threatened by China and Russia.

video:video.video:title: Powerful storm strikes Romania ’s capital, killing one person and causing flooding
video:video.video:description: A powerful storm struck Romania ’s capital overnight into Wednesday, killing one person and causing flooding in 20 counties. (AP Video by Nick Dumitrache)

video:video.video:title: World Cup buzz has the potential to mint more soccer fans among kids — some just toddlers now
video:video.video:description: Across the country, experts are watching to see whether the World Cup will give a boost to youth soccer and mint fans for decades to come.

video:video.video:title: Celebrations erupt at San Diego watch party as Mexico tops Ecuador, 2-0
video:video.video:description: The crowd at a World Cup watch party in San Diego, California erupted in raucous celebrations on Tuesday night as Mexico topped Ecuador 2-0 in a World Cup round of 32 soccer match.

video:video.video:title: 'I don't look like a desert creature, and yet I'm always there:' Anya Taylor-Joy reunited with her favorite filming location in Apple TV's 'Lucky'
video:video.video:description: Anya Taylor-Joy makes her way back to the desert in Apple TV's "Lucky." (Jul 2)

video:video.video:title: 'Miracle' dog found alive in Venezuela after 8 days trapped under rubble
video:video.video:description: A 6-year-old dog named Buddy was pulled alive on Thursday, after spending eight agonizing days trapped beneath his collapsed home in the Venezuelan city of Caraballeda (AP Video shot by Fernanda Pesce, Matias Delacroix and Miguel Malliotakis).

video:video.video:title: Ringo Starr can't quit touring: 'I'm out again'
video:video.video:description: On his 86th birthday, Ringo Starr told The Associated Press that despite repeatedly telling his family each tour would be his last, he keeps returning to the road because music remains central to his life.

video:video.video:title: More US children have been drowning in recent years
video:video.video:description: Child drowning deaths rose from about 750 in 2019 to more than 860 in 2024, the most recent year for which complete data is available.

video:video.video:title: Midnight Til Morning love One Direction comparisons
video:video.video:description: Midnight Til Morning were compared to One Direction by Liam Payne himself, and say his fans give them continuous support

video:video.video:title: 3 people die of asphyxiation during Mexico City celebrations after World Cup win
video:video.video:description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum expressed her condolences on Wednesday after three people died of asphyxiation during massive celebrations in downtown Mexico City after the national team’s victory over Ecuador in the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: A California farmer and a food distributor battle over a fruit patent
video:video.video:description: California farmer Cesar Mora said he couldn’t bear to see his harvest of nectar fall to the ground and rot for a second year, so he is giving it away.

video:video.video:title: Trump insists he doesn't manage his investments after $1.2B crypto earnings disclosure
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Wednesday he is not involved in managing his finances, insisting outside funds handle his investments.

video:video.video:title: The Associated Press is reporting outside Taylor Swift's wedding
video:video.video:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding is slated to begin at 5 p.m. Friday at New York’s Madison Square Garden and could last until 4 a.m. the next morning.

video:video.video:title: Funeral preparations are underway in Tehran for Iran's slain supreme leader
video:video.video:description: The Iranian capital of Tehran is preparing for a dayslong funeral for former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

video:video.video:title: 'SOS Venezuela' projected on Christ the Redeemer Statue in Rio
video:video.video:description: 'SOS Venezuela' projected on Christ the Redeemer Statue in Rio

video:video.video:title: NATO readies for 'big reveal' on arms deals to prove firepower to Trump
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump and NATO leaders are meeting in Ankara in Turkey on Tuesday for a two-day summit focused on the military alliance's future.

video:video.video:title: Democratic socialist Melat Kiros defeats longtime US House incumbent in Colorado
video:video.video:description: Democratic socialist Melat Kiros beat U.S. Rep. Diana DeGette in a Colorado House primary Tuesday, a stunning victory for the first-time candidate against a nearly 30-year incumbent and another win for progressive challengers across the country.

video:video.video:title: Trump filing shows he took in about $1.2 billion from crypto businesses last year
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump took in nearly $1.2 billion dollars from his crypto businesses last year, a federal filing shows. (Produced by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: Officials condemn Coney Island shooting that wounded 8, including children
video:video.video:description: Police said Sunday morning that a 21-year-old woman was in critical condition while the others are expected to survive.

video:video.video:title: Parisians embrace swimming in the Seine in the shadow of the Eiffel Tower
video:video.video:description: The Seine was a venue for some swimming and triathlon competition during the Paris 2024 Olympics, which accelerated a multibillion-euro cleanup of the river through major sewer upgrades, new rainwater storage infrastructure and other projects designed to reduce pollution.

video:video.video:title: Global fans embrace Lucha Libre in Mexico City during World Cup
video:video.video:description: Travelers from all over the world have succumbed to the magic of Mexico’s "Lucha Libre," which, alongside soccer, is a national passion. (AP video shot by Marko Alvarez)

video:video.video:title: AI stocks resume their drops and drag markets lower worldwide
video:video.video:description: The roller-coaster ride for AI stocks snapped lower again and weighed on Wall Street. The S&P 500 fell 0.4% Tuesday, even though the majority of stocks within the index rose.

video:video.video:title: 'The Odyssey' stars take over London for world premiere
video:video.video:description: Matt Damon, Tom Holland, Zendaya, Anne Hathaway, Robert Pattinson and Lupita Nyong'o attended the world premiere of "The Odyssey" in London

video:video.video:title: Midnight Til Morning have upgraded their live show (but not their dancing)
video:video.video:description: Midnight Til Morning say fans can expect to see an elevated performance during the band's next tour - but don't expect their dancing to have improved

video:video.video:title: AP Top stories July 2
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, July 2nd: Record-breaking heat grips Midwest and East Coast; U.S. employers hired 57,000 people in June; Russian aerial attack kills more than 20 in Ukraine; Man rescued in Venezuela after eight days beneath rubble.

video:video.video:title: Ghana and Colombia fans confident ahead of World Cup matchup in Kansas City
video:video.video:description: The first Round of 32 World Cup match in Kansas City will be played on Friday and fans gathered to rally for their teams. (AP video by Mark Vancleave & Nicholas Ingram)

video:video.video:title: Children found in ‘deplorable’ Ohio home were part of same family
video:video.video:description: Officials say 16 children found living in “deplorable” conditions inside an Ohio home are part of the same family.

video:video.video:title: What we know about Taylor Swift's wedding
video:video.video:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce will celebrate their wedding at Madison Square Garden on Friday night, according to a law enforcement official briefed on the security plans.

video:video.video:title: Lexi and Reese share Stella McCartney dress
video:video.video:description: Lexi Minetree says "it's crazy" she got to wear Reese Witherspoon's Stella McCartney dress from the 2014 Met Gala at the premiere of her TV show "Elle" in London

video:video.video:title: Judge dismisses Prince Harry's privacy invasion lawsuit against publisher of Daily Mail
video:video.video:description: Prince Harry's final lawsuit aimed at taming the British tabloids ended in defeat Tuesday as a judge said he failed to prove his privacy invasion claims against the publisher of the Daily Mail.

video:video.video:title: Russian attack on Ukraine shakes Kyiv for hours
video:video.video:description: Russian attack on Ukraine shakes Kyiv for hours

video:video.video:title: Palestinians across Gaza Strip reflect on pre-war lives, marking 1,000 days of war
video:video.video:description: One thousand days have passed since the Hamas-led attack on Israel sparked the war in Gaza. Other conflicts have emerged in the region, and fragile ceasefires show scars of persistent attacks.

video:video.video:title: Matt Damon attended rowing camp for 'The Odyssey' preparation
video:video.video:description: 'The Odyssey' star Matt Damon says his role for the film was "not a standard acting job." (July 7)

video:video.video:title: Visitors reflect on America at 250 as they view original Declaration of Independence
video:video.video:description: Americans visiting the nation's capital are pondering the history and future of their country as they view an original founding document signed 250 years ago on July 4th, the Declaration of Independence.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors argue the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk should stand trial
video:video.video:description: The weeklong preliminary hearing marks the most significant presentation of evidence in the case so far.

video:video.video:title: Truck carrying Khamenei's casket surrounded by crowd of mourners in Qom
video:video.video:description: Mourners dressed in black gathered just south of Qom in Iran for a for late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral service, with throngs of people surrounding the truck carrying his coffin.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters try to contain fast-moving wildfire in Greece
video:video.video:description: Firefighters try to contain fast-moving wildfire in Greece

video:video.video:title: From Taylor's wedding dress to haute couture for Jonathan Anderson
video:video.video:description: Only days after Taylor Swift wore her Dior wedding dress, designer Jonathan Anderson showcased his new Dior haute couture show to stars like Priyanka Chopra Jonas, Ruth Negga and Naomi Watts on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Ten days after twin earthquakes, Venezuelans continue retrieving bodies of missing loved ones from rubble
video:video.video:description: Ten days after two powerful earthquakes shook Venezuela, families across the devastated north-central coast are still clawing through mountains of pulverized concrete with their bare hands to locate thousands of relatives missing in the ruins.

video:video.video:title: Fans cheer as U.S. beats Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 to keep its World Cup dreams alive
video:video.video:description: Folarin Balogun scored his third goal of the World Cup before being sent off with a red card in the second half, and Malik Tillman converted on a free kick to give the 10-man United States squad a 2-0 win over Bosnia-Herzegovina to advance to the round of 16.
video:video.video:tag: Bosnia and Herzegovina National Soccer Team

video:video.video:title: Indonesian president meets India's Modi in Jakarta for talks on expanding economic and defense ties
video:video.video:description: The visit marks Modi’s second trip to Indonesia as prime minister, following his previous visit in 2018, and comes after Prabowo attended India’s Republic Day celebrations as chief guest last year, Sugiono, who like many Indonesians uses a single name.

video:video.video:title: Drone footage captures Venezuelans retrieving bodies of missing loved ones from rubble
video:video.video:description: Ten days after two earthquakes shook Venezuela, families across the devastated north-central coast are still clawing through mountains of pulverized concrete with their bare hands to locate thousands of relatives missing in the ruins.

video:video.video:title: Cape Verde soccer players given grand welcome on return home from first World Cup
video:video.video:description: With three draws, the Blue Sharks advanced to the round of 32, an unprecedented achievement for the small African nation, where they were defeated by Argentina in extra time.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire in eastern Spain forces evacuation of 500 residents from homes
video:video.video:description: A wildfire in Castellon, Valencia, forced evacuation of 500 residents from their homes. Regional authorities were battling a wildfire in Soneja in eastern Spain after it broke out Sunday afternoon.

video:video.video:title: Three injured in Russian strike on Kramatorsk as Ukraine says it downs 130 drones
video:video.video:description: Three people were injured in a Russian airstrike on a residential area of Kramatorsk in Ukraine's Donetsk region.

video:video.video:title: View of destruction in Khan Younis, marking 1,000 days of war in Gaza
video:video.video:description: View of destruction in Khan Younis, marking 1,000 days of war in Gaza

video:video.video:title: Hospital in France gears up for the next heat wave armed with lessons learnt from previous ones
video:video.video:description: At a Paris-region hospital, emergency medics needed it to plunge patients into cold-water baths to speedily bring down their temperatures so they wouldn't join the growing tally of dead from a record-smashing heat wave.

video:video.video:title: Trump seeks to enforce spending promises at NATO summit
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump heads to Ankara, Turkey, on Monday evening for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed alliance allies to spend more on defense.

video:video.video:title: Mourners at Khamenei funeral procession in Tehran vow 'revenge'
video:video.video:description: A truck carrying Iranian late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's flag-draped coffin, crept through crowds of black-clad mourners in Tehran, who reached out to touch the grating.

video:video.video:title: Anti-immigration protesters march in South Africa
video:video.video:description: Thousands of demonstrators gathered in parts of South Africa to rally against illegal immigration in the biggest migration-related protests since the wave of anti-migrant violence in 2008.

video:video.video:title: Explosions wound at least 18 in Syria's capital during Macron visit
video:video.video:description: Explosions rocked the Syrian capital Damascus on Tuesday, wounding at least 18 people as French President Emmanuel Macron met with his Syrian counterpart in a landmark visit, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Fierce winds hit US Pacific territories as Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall near Guam
video:video.video:description: Super Typhoon Bavi made landfall on Monday, over a tiny U.S. territorial island in the western Pacific near Guam, bringing powerful winds and torrential rain to the Northern Mariana Islands.

video:video.video:title: Anxiety mounts for residents as Western wildfires continue
video:video.video:description: Residents across a number of states are facing uncertainty as wildfires spread across the West. (AP Video: Aya Diab)

video:video.video:title: Coffin of late Supreme Leader Khamenei paraded through Tehran
video:video.video:description: The coffin of late Supreme Leader Khamenei, and those of his family killed on Feb. 28 in an airstrike at the start of the war launched by Israel and the United States, were carried on a truck through a massive crowd of mourners in Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Venezuelan security guard pulled alive from building basement 8 days after twin quakes
video:video.video:description: Rescuers pulled a 43-year-old security guard alive from a collapsed basement early Thursday, ending a grueling days-long operation that became a symbol of hope after the devastation of twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela eight days earlier.

video:video.video:title: Brazilian fans and Japanese descendants celebrated a dramatic 2-1 comeback victory over Japan
video:video.video:description: While emotions ran high in São Paulo's Liberdade district, celebrations took on a different scale in Rio de Janeiro, where Copacabana Beach became a sea of yellow and green after Gabriel Martinelli scored the winning goal deep into injury time, sealing Brazil's comeback victory.

video:video.video:title: Australian officials ask fans to respect the privacy of Neil, a 1-ton seal who respects nothing
video:video.video:description: In June, a bellowing and blubbery 5-year-old elephant seal hauled himself onto land for his twice-yearly tour of beachside towns in southern Tasmania after months of feeding at sea.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia surprises in a 9-year-old soccer player with July 4 World Cup tickets
video:video.video:description: Ben Snyder, a passionate young soccer player from Philadelphia, beamed with a radiant smile when he learned that he and his family would be attending the World Cup match on Independence Day.

video:video.video:title: Mexicans resume daily life after another World Cup exit
video:video.video:description: Mexicans woke Monday to newspaper headlines marking the end of the national team's World Cup run after a 3-2 loss to England in the round of 16 ended hopes of a historic tournament on home soil.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 1
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, July 1st: Lawyers for E. Jean Carroll demand $5 million from Trump; Supreme Court rejects Trump’s birthright citizenship limits; Democratic Socialist wins Colorado U.S.

video:video.video:title: Israeli military close entrances to West Bank town, disrupting residents' lives
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the town of Sinjil in the Israeli-occupied West Bank woke up Wednesday to find checkpoints to the village closed by Israeli troops, upending plans and disrupting daily lives and schedules.

video:video.video:title: Cuba's public healthcare system in crisis amid U.S. embargo
video:video.video:description: After two surgeries and several rounds of radiation therapy over the past four years to treat a tumor, Irisleydis Tristá has spent the past seven months unable to get a CT scan to determine whether the cancer has grown or spread.

video:video.video:title: Stars at Schiaparelli as Haute Couture Fall/Winter begins
video:video.video:description: Emma Corrin, Michelle Yeoh and Bad Bunny are among the guests at the Schiaparelli Fall/Winter collection in Paris.

video:video.video:title: Residents face scenes of destruction as Russia launches a massive attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: Russia has launched a massive drone and missile attack on Kyiv, killing at least 20 civilians in the city and injuring many more. (AP video shot by: Dan Bashakov)

video:video.video:title: New England ice supplier describes joy and challenges of higher demand sparked by extreme heat
video:video.video:description: Extreme heat baking large parts of the U.S. generates extra business – and grueling working conditions – for New England ice vendor ahead of Fourth of July festivities in a hub of country's independence (AP Video and Production: Rodrique Ngowi.)

video:video.video:title: Alabama teen's shark attack inspires national alert system
video:video.video:description: Lulu Gribbin, who survived a near-fatal shark attack in 2024, has inspired federal legislation authorizing shark bite alerts.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 1
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, July 1st: Trump flies on new Air Force One gifted by Qatar; Data shows Trump made $1.2 billion from crypto; Ship runs aground in Strait of Hormuz, Iran says; Traditionalist Catholics defy Pope Leo with bishop consecration.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy warns Russia preparing new large-scale attack on Ukraine
video:video.video:description: Hours after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned on Sunday that Russia is going to launch a new large-scale attack on the country, Russia launched waves of missiles and drones targeting Kyiv overnight into Monday that killed at least seven people, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Police break up Tibet protest outside Chinese Embassy in India
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Tibetan Youth Congress activists protested outside the Chinese Embassy in New Delhi on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: FIFA lifts US star striker red card suspension at World Cup after Trump calls Infantino
video:video.video:description: Folarin Balogun received a red card for stepping awkwardly on the right ankle of Tarik Muharemović of Bosnia-Herzegovina in a 2-0 round of 32 win on Wednesday, triggering an automatic one-game suspension.

video:video.video:title: Starmer says Farage is calling by-election because he is 'up to his neck in sleaze'
video:video.video:description: "This is a desperate stunt from Nigel Farage, and it's obvious why he's doing it. He is up to his neck in sleaze," UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said in Ankara, where he is attending a NATO summit.

video:video.video:title: Prince Harry's UK trip sparks media buzz over whether Meghan and kids will join him
video:video.video:description: The drama that seems to surround Prince Harry returns to the U.K. this week and the previews already have the British press buzzing with anticipation.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese animal shelter takes in pets abandoned by war, displaced by Israeli invasion
video:video.video:description: In the village of Kafroue in southern Lebanon, Hussein Hamza makes his daily rounds to feed and check on his furry and feathered charges.

video:video.video:title: Hemingway’s masterpiece on Spain’s bull runs turns 100 years old
video:video.video:description: This year’s San Fermin festival in Spain marked the 100th anniversary of the publication of Ernest Hemingway’s book “The Sun Also Rises” that launched the future Nobel Laureate to literary fame and put Pamplona on the map for millions of people around the world.

video:video.video:title: Russian attack kills at least 20 and causes damage across Kyiv
video:video.video:description: Loud explosions shook the Ukrainian capital for hours during the night, with many people sheltering in subway stations after authorities issued air raid warnings.

video:video.video:title: Indian Prime Minister welcomes Japanese Prime Minister to New Delhi
video:video.video:description: Japan's leader was in India to meet with the country's prime minister. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi was greeted in New Delhi by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

video:video.video:title: Drone show lights up night sky over Philadelphia's Independence Hall
video:video.video:description: Philadelphia held a drone show outside Independence Hall to highlight the upcoming Fourth of July weekend in the United States.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter describes scene on the street near Taylor Swift's wedding
video:video.video:description: Fans lined the streets of New York Friday, looking to catch a glimpse of guests heading in to the wedding of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce at Madison Square Garden wedding.

video:video.video:title: Vance says small, but loud voices will speak of America's imperfections on its birthday
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance spoke aboard the USS Kearsarge in New York Harbor on July 4th and said some small but loud voices would speak "obsessively" on America's birthday about its imperfections instead of its greatness.

video:video.video:title: Fans of K-pop supergroup BTS march in Chile over venue uncertainty
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans of South Korean group BTS marched through downtown Santiago on Sunday to demand confirmation of the venue for the band’s upcoming performances.

video:video.video:title: When is the best time to buy a house? Business basics: mortgage rates
video:video.video:description: Mortgage rates play a big role in what you'll pay each month for your home. But with rates constantly changing, waiting for the "perfect" rate could mean missing the right opportunity to buy.

video:video.video:title: Onlookers react to banner atop Empire State Building
video:video.video:description: Two people got to the top of the Empire State Building’s antenna and unfurled a banner about “the power of love” at midday Wednesday, before descending, embracing, taking selfies and ultimately being arrested.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 6
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday July 6th: Iran begins funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei; Ukraine says Russia launched massive missile and drone strike; Deaths in New Jersey blamed on heat; FIFA lifts red card suspension for U.S. star player Folarin Balogun.
video:video.video:tag: Bosnia and Herzegovina National Soccer Team

video:video.video:title: Chinese men in Germany used Telegram to share rape videos and drugging tips
video:video.video:description: A German investigation into an online predator network that thrived on Telegram for years has led to the convictions of three alleged members of the group's inner circle and the ongoing trial of a fourth in Berlin.

video:video.video:title: Le Pen's future as French presidential contender hinges on Paris appeals court ruling
video:video.video:description: Marine Le Pen and other members of her National Rally party were found guilty of using money intended for parliamentary assistants to pay party staff between 2004 and 2016.

video:video.video:title: Man escapes apartment fire through neighbor's window in Antwerp
video:video.video:description: Dramatic footage showed a man escaping from a fire at an apartment block on the outskirts of the Belgian city of Antwerp, climbing from a balcony to a nearby window.

video:video.video:title: Mbappé condemns Paraguyan senator over racist remarks after World Cup match
video:video.video:description: France star Kylian Mbappé condemned Celeste Amarilla, a Paraguayan senator, over racist remarks she made following Paraguay’s loss to France in the round of 16 at the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Riding through history on Switzerland's Furka steam railway
video:video.video:description: Train enthusiasts will celebrate the 100th anniversary of a historic Swiss railway this weekend as vintage steam locomotives ferry tourists through the stunning Alpine scenery as part of the celebration.

video:video.video:title: Father finds missing daughter under rubble as Venezuelans unearth loved ones without state help
video:video.video:description: After 11 days of using just his hands to dig through the muck and rubble of the twin earthquakes that devastated his public housing development in northern Venezuela, Alexander, dazed and sleep-deprived, reached the last missing member of his family member on Sunday — his 12-year-old daughter.

video:video.video:title: Italy displays paintings from an ancient Etruscan tomb, its latest cultural acquisition
video:video.video:description: Italy on Tuesday put on display one of the best known examples of Etruscan painting, from a tomb that has become the latest acquisition in the Culture Ministry’s buying spree of big-ticket pieces of the country’s cultural heritage.

video:video.video:title: Trump administration silent on Iranian school strike four months later
video:video.video:description: It was the deadliest reported strike in the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran, and most of the victims were children.

video:video.video:title: 100 years on, Hemingway's 'The Sun Also Rises' still inspires Americans to run with the bulls
video:video.video:description: Ernest Hemingway's 1926 novel 'Fiesta: The Sun Also Rises' captivated generations of readers with its story of exotic travel and the pursuit of impossible love.

video:video.video:title: Reform UK’s Nigel Farage says he’ll quit as a lawmaker and seek re-election to clear name over financial allegations
video:video.video:description: The unexpected resignation is an effort by the anti-immigration politician to preempt a standards investigation into undeclared and potentially rule-breaking donations that could have seen him ejected as a lawmaker, and to present himself as the victim of a witch hunt by the media and his political

video:video.video:title: Sons of late Iran Supreme Leader Khamenei among mourners at his funeral
video:video.video:description: Khamenei's sons Masoud, Meysam and Mostafa, as well as Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian, Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf and Revolutionary Guard head Gen.

video:video.video:title: Federal Reserve Chair Warsh emphasizes political independence, signals focus on inflation
video:video.video:description: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that the central bank would remain independent and seek to bring down inflation, likely foreclosing the rate cuts President Donald Trump has sought.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors argue the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk should stand trial
video:video.video:description: A five-day preliminary hearing for the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk is underway in Utah.

video:video.video:title: Sirens sound in Kuwait after it says its air defences intercepted incoming fire
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded in Kuwait on Wednesday as the country's air defenses engaged to intercept incoming fire after the United States launched airstrikes targeting Iran.

video:video.video:title: Olivia Wilde reacts to England win
video:video.video:description: Olivia Wilde reacts to England win

video:video.video:title: From a little house to a bigger stage, Motown Museum readies its $75M expansion
video:video.video:description: A $75 million expansion of the Motown Museum in Detroit is entering its final phases ahead of a grand opening next year. (AP video: Mike Householder and Corey Williams)

video:video.video:title: Fans gather outside Madison Square Garden for Taylor Swift's wedding
video:video.video:description: "It's our version of a royal wedding." Taylor Swift fans gather outside Madison Square Garden for the pop megastar's marriage to Travis Kelce.

video:video.video:title: Food writer Anissa Helou lifts the lid on her culinary inspirations
video:video.video:description: Before becoming one of the Middle East’s most acclaimed cooks and food writers, Anissa Helou had no intention of either path.

video:video.video:title: From Chocolate Avenue to the World Cup, how Hershey, Pennsylvania, shaped Christian Pulisic
video:video.video:description: Standout U.S. national soccer team player Christian Pulisic hails from Hershey, Pennsylvania. Known as the “Sweetest Place on Earth” thanks to its chocolate-drenched origins.

video:video.video:title: Iran mourners honor late supreme leader in seminary city of Qom
video:video.video:description: Iranian state television early Tuesday aired live images of tens of thousands of people walking toward Jamkaran Mosque, just south of Qom, for a funeral service for Khamenei.

video:video.video:title: Ringo Starr celebrates 86th birthday
video:video.video:description: Ringo Starr was joined by his wife, Barbara Bach, and friends in Beverly Hills on Tuesday to celebrate his 86th birthday.

video:video.video:title: Protests as far-right Alternative for Germany holds convention, eyeing gains in regional elections
video:video.video:description: Thousands of protesters gathered on Saturday as Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) held its national convention in Erfurt.

video:video.video:title: 'Operation Hard Ball' nets 24 arrests targeting international India-based organized crime gangs
video:video.video:description: Law enforcement officials at federal, local, and international agencies charged 37 alleged members of India-based transnational organized crime groups with multiple criminal acts, including the assassination in Canada of a prominent Indian activist.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he called FIFA president after 'horrible' red card on U.S. striker
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday he called FIFA President Gianni Infantino to review the red card given to U.S. men's soccer striker Folarin Balogun, calling it a "horrible" call.

video:video.video:title: Attorney says autopsy of 1-year-old shot by Mississippi police conflicts with officers' account
video:video.video:description: A Mississippi family whose 1-year-old child was killed when officers fired on a car are challenging police claims about the shooting.

video:video.video:title: Marine heat wave off California coast leaves seabirds starving or dead
video:video.video:description: Many seabirds are starving to death as a marine heat wave lingers off California and fish seek deeper, cooler waters, scientists say.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US considering selling F-35 jets to Turkey despite ban
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. will lift sanctions on Turkey and consider restoring the sale of F-35 fighter jets to the country, signaling a possible reversal of a ban his administration imposed after Ankara bought Russian missile defense systems.

video:video.video:title: Russia's missile and drone attack on Ukraine kills at least 18
video:video.video:description: All of the ballistic missiles launched by Russia struck their targets, underscoring Kyiv’s need for more Patriot interceptor missiles — a point Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will likely reiterate at a NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, this week.

video:video.video:title: Falling brick and buckling columns trigger evacuations around Manhattan high-rise
video:video.video:description: The New York Fire Department said on Tuesday that it received reports of bricks falling from the 38-story building at 235 East 42nd Street at around 8 a.m.

video:video.video:title: Panda birthdays at Hong Kong Zoo
video:video.video:description: A group of pandas in Hong Kong are celebrating their birthdays at the amusement park where they live.

video:video.video:title: Norway's crown prince joins vast crowd in Oslo celebrating victory over Brazil with viking row
video:video.video:description: Norwegian Crown Prince Haakon joined thousands of Norwegian fans to celebrate the country's historic victory at the FIFA World Cup's round of 16.

video:video.video:title: Spanish firefighters reinforce efforts to tackle wildfires in central Portugal
video:video.video:description: The wildfire had burned across an area of 12,000 hectares (29,653 acres) by Sunday. More than 1,200 firefighters backed up by nearly 400 vehicles and 15 aircraft tried to put out a blaze that broke out on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Emma Corrin, Michelle Yeoh and Bad Bunny arrive at Schiaparelli
video:video.video:description: A-lists guests arriving to see the Schiaparelli Haute Couture Fall/Winter 2026-2027 Women's collection presented in Paris include Emma Corrin, Michelle Yeoh, and Bad Bunny.

video:video.video:title: Investigators at scene of deadly cafe explosion in Damascus
video:video.video:description: Syria’s Health Ministry says an explosive device has detonated in a Damascus cafe, killing at least nine people and wounding 20 others.

video:video.video:title: Police at scene after explosion in Monaco, witness recounts moments after blast
video:video.video:description: Police were at the scene in Monaco on Tuesday after an explosive went off in an apartment building entrance, wounding three people, reportedly including a Ukrainian tycoon with ties to Russia.

video:video.video:title: Maine couple snaps photo of a bear chasing a moose calf
video:video.video:description: A married couple in Maine saw a large bear chasing a calf moose down a rural roadway and were able to capture some photos of the chase. (AP Video: Dylan Lovan)

video:video.video:title: Le Pen's future as presidential contender hinges on appeals court ruling
video:video.video:description: Le Pen has challenged a March 2025 ruling that found her and other members of her National Rally party guilty of misusing European Parliament funds.

video:video.video:title: 3 firefighters killed in Colorado remembered for their bravery as wildfires churn in the west of US
video:video.video:description: Wildland firefighters gathered Sunday to pay tribute to three of their own who died after they were trapped by flames in Colorado a week ago.

video:video.video:title: Bernie Sanders joins Democrats’ calls for Graham Platner to drop out of Maine Senate race
video:video.video:description: A woman who previously dated Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner said he drunkenly forced her to have sex after she told him to stop, leading prominent supporters to pull their endorsements and throwing a must-win race for the party into turmoil.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 3
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday July 3RD: Iran holds funeral for late Supreme Leader; Iran warns oil tankers to use its approved routes in Strait of Hormuz; Venezuelan acting president defends earthquake response; East Coast heat wave to continue through weekend.

video:video.video:title: Fire rips through an Ohio motel, killing 3 who were trapped in a room
video:video.video:description: Wooster Township Fire Chief said firefighters made several attempts to reach the people who were trapped.

video:video.video:title: Major Russian drone and missile attack kills 17 in Kyiv
video:video.video:description: Russia hammered Kyiv in a major drone and missile attack overnight into Thursday morning, killing at least 17 civilians and injuring many more in what Moscow described as retaliation for Ukrainian strikes on oil facilities that have caused fuel shortages and put pressure on President Vladimir Putin.

video:video.video:title: Police investigate a shopping mall shooting that left 2 dead in suburban Detroit
video:video.video:description: Three people were shot and two of them died after a fight between two groups of young people at a shopping mall in the city of Dearborn, Michigan.

video:video.video:title: Dayslong funeral for late Supreme Leader Khamenei begins in Iran
video:video.video:description: An outdoor stage set up at the Grand Mosalla resembled the stage where Khamenei once gave his speeches at a husseiniyah at his compound in downtown Tehran.

video:video.video:title: San Francisco turns red, white, and blue as US soccer fans arrive ahead of World Cup knockout
video:video.video:description: Supporters of the United States men's national soccer team are arriving across the San Francisco Bay Area ahead of Wednesday’s FIFA World Cup knockout match against Bosnia and Herzegovina.
video:video.video:tag: Bosnia and Herzegovina National Soccer Team

video:video.video:title: Three-year-old boy rescued from Venezuela quake rubble after being trapped for 6 days
video:video.video:description: A toddler who had been trapped for six days under a collapsed building in Venezuela was rescued Tuesday, National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez said.

video:video.video:title: Hearing for the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk pushes forward
video:video.video:description: The weeklong preliminary hearing for the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk has entered its second day.

video:video.video:title: Scorching July heat deepens suffering in Gaza's displacement camps
video:video.video:description: Palestinians have to resort to ancient methods to keep cool amid blistering heat, as Israeli airstrikes destroyed large parts of Gaza’s energy infrastructure.

video:video.video:title: Paraguay senator on racist remarks targeting France captain Kylian Mbappé
video:video.video:description: Paraguay senator Celeste Amarilla on Tuesday said France had no basis to pursue a case against her, days after she posted allegedly racist comments online after soccer star Kylian Mbappé converted the winning penalty in France’s victory over Paraguay in the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: 16 children rescued from Ohio home were ‘almost feral,’ authorities say
video:video.video:description: Authorities say 16 children from the same family who were rescued from a run-down home in rural Ohio were living in horrific conditions and were confined to just one room over much of the past four years.

video:video.video:title: Will the World Cup spark soccer interest in kids?
video:video.video:description: The buzz around the FIFA World Cup has the potential to mint more soccer fans among kids — some just toddlers now.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's building a White House helipad
video:video.video:description: The president says he's building a granite helipad on the White House lawn. The project, estimated to cost up to $6 million, is said to be privately funded.

video:video.video:title: Hemingway's 'The Sun Also Rises' still inspires Americans to run with the bulls
video:video.video:description: Hemingway's 'The Sun Also Rises' still inspires Americans to run with the bulls

video:video.video:title: Three-year-old boy rescued from Venezuela quake rubble after being trapped for 6 days
video:video.video:description: The sole survivor rescued in Venezuela by Tuesday afternoon was a toddler who had been trapped for six days under a collapsed building, National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez said.

video:video.video:title: Dog found alive in Venezuela after 8 days trapped under rubble
video:video.video:description: A 6-year-old dog named Buddy was pulled alive on Thursday, after spending eight agonizing days trapped beneath his collapsed home in the Venezuelan city of Caraballeda.

video:video.video:title: Scratching that bug bite might feel good at first but science explains why it’s a bad idea
video:video.video:description: You’ve likely heard it since childhood: Don’t scratch that bug bite or rash, you’ll make it worse. But why would something that feels so good be bad?

video:video.video:title: The world's largest digital camera begins decade-long survey of the universe
video:video.video:description: The largest digital camera ever built is starting to capture images of unseen corners of the universe. The Vera C.

video:video.video:title: The family of a man shot by the Tennessee National Guard demands release of video
video:video.video:description: The grandfather of a man who was fatally shot by Tennessee National Guard in Memphis says he wants answers from investigators.

video:video.video:title: Midrange drones are reshaping the battlefield for Ukraine
video:video.video:description: By repeatedly striking the highways that carry fuel, ammunition and reinforcements, Ukrainian commanders say they have stalled Russian advances and enabled Ukrainian counterattacks and strikes into illegally annexed Crimea to isolate the peninsula from the mainland.

video:video.video:title: Demonstration in Istanbul against the NATO Summit
video:video.video:description: Thousands of protestors from leftist parties, syndicates and women’s groups on Tuesday marched in central Istanbul against the NATO summit at the end of the first day of the conference.

video:video.video:title: BTS brings the ARIRANG to London
video:video.video:description: K-Pop superstars played the first of two nights in London, as part of their ARIRANG world tour. (July 7)

video:video.video:title: Staff struggle to keep UK's outdated school buildings cool in record heat
video:video.video:description: Many schools are built to keep warm in cold winters and not designed for current climate conditions, leaving staff struggling to conduct classes safely.

video:video.video:title: 'HOPE' director describes daily drinking sessions with Michael Fassbender during filming
video:video.video:description: Director Na Hong-jin looks back on the making of his sci-fi monster movie "HOPE," which features Michael Fassbender and Alicia Vikander as extraterrestrials. (July 6)

video:video.video:title: Former Olympian indicted on felony charge in what Trump called Reflecting Pool vandalism
video:video.video:description: Former Olympian indicted on felony charge in what Trump called Reflecting Pool vandalism

video:video.video:title: Summer swimming returns to Paris with 3 bathing sites opened on the Seine
video:video.video:description: Swimming in the Seine is settling into a new Paris summer ritual as the city opened three supervised bathing sites on Saturday for the second year in a row.

video:video.video:title: Maher jokes White House called his Mark Twain Prize "fake news"
video:video.video:description: Before accepting the Mark Twain Prize, Bill Maher poked fun at the White House, which had labeled news of his selection for the honor as "fake news" when it was announced.

video:video.video:title: Overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in southwest Pakistan, killing 40 passengers
video:video.video:description: A passenger bus plunged from a highway into a rocky ravine in southwestern Pakistan early Friday, killing 40 people and injuring eight others in one of the deadliest road accidents in recent years, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Retrofitted Qatari jet takes flight as Air Force One for Trump's trip to North Dakota
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday took his maiden voyage on a new Air Force One — a retrofitted Boeing 747-800 worth $400 million gifted by Qatar.

video:video.video:title: New York bar owner calls street closures and added security for Swift-Kelce wedding ‘ridiculous’
video:video.video:description: Co-owner of O'Briens Bar and Grill located across from Madison Square Garden questioned if the couple can afford the permits to close the streets and a “big lavish ceremony” then why not also buy out the nearby businesses so they are not adversely affected.

video:video.video:title: US launches new strikes on Iran, revokes oil sales permit after 3 ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military attacked Iran early Wednesday after it said Tehran struck three ships in the Strait of Hormuz, part of an American effort that also revoked the Islamic Republic's ability to openly sell crude oil in the world market.

video:video.video:title: Virtual taekwondo set to take center stage at Asian Games
video:video.video:description: Virtual taekwondo is kicking its way into the mainstream as its popularity grows across Southeast Asia.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters battle wildfires in Ukraine's Chernobyl exclusion zone
video:video.video:description: The Ukraine State Emergency Service said radiation levels in the exclusion zone remained within normal limits.

video:video.video:title: Fireworks display in Philadelphia marks 250th anniversary of US independence, following weather delay
video:video.video:description: Crowds gathered in Philadelphia early Sunday to watch the fireworks celebrating the 250th anniversary of American independence.

video:video.video:title: Extreme heat shuts down the Great American State Fair in DC for a few hours
video:video.video:description: Extreme heat in the U.S. is impacting festivities commemorating the 250th anniversary of American independence, including a temporary closure of the Great American State Fair in Washington D.C.

video:video.video:title: Thursday marks 1,000 days since Hamas-led attack on Israel sparked war in Gaza
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and people in Israel are marking 1,000 days of war. It's the 1,000th day since Hamas militants carried out their attack in Israel on October 7, 2023.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong bookseller Lam Wing-kee dies in Taipei, aged 70
video:video.video:description: Lam Wing-kee, a former bookseller in Hong Kong who became a symbol of resistance to Beijing's crackdown on speech freedom after he was seized by Chinese authorities in late 2015, has died in Taiwan, the island's official Central News Agency reported, citing an unnamed source.

video:video.video:title: Robotic elephants draw crowds and controversy in some of India's Hindu temples
video:video.video:description: In southern India, robotic elephants are replacing live ones in Hindu rituals and festivals following concerns over animal abuse and fatal elephant rampages.
video:video.video:tag: People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals

video:video.video:title: Spain en route to quarterfinals after ending Cristiano Ronaldo’s World Cup career
video:video.video:description: Mikel Merino scored in the first minute of second-half stoppage time, and Spain beat Portugal 1-0 on Monday to end the World Cup career of superstar Cristiano Ronaldo.

video:video.video:title: Should you leave your air conditioner on all day?
video:video.video:description: Is it better to shut off your air conditioner when you leave, or keep it running? Experts say the answer isn’t one-size-fits-all. (AP Video by Kiki Sideris)

video:video.video:title: Spectacular orange lava flows from Mount Etna, Europe's largest active volcano
video:video.video:description: An eruption of Mount Etna, on the Italian island of Sicily, produced a spectacular lava show that turned part of Europe's largest active volcano orange.
